versions.note: The shipped model — but measured on clinical-lens images
The same model on the photos the app really sees — the drop
Recovered on phone photos — the honest, deployable number

changed: Tested the shipped dermoscopy-trained model on PAD-UFES-20 — 2,298 real smartphone photos of skin lesions, split by patient (969 patients, 1,564 images after excluding actinic keratosis to match v1's benign/malignant label space).
Fine-tuned that same model on the smartphone photos and re-evaluated on the identical held-out test split, so the only thing that changes between the two numbers is the training domain.
improved: Measured the domain gap honestly: on phone photos the dermoscopy model dropped from ROC-AUC 0.914 to 0.743, its specificity collapsing to 32% — it flagged roughly two-thirds of benign lesions.

Fine-tuning recovered it to ROC-AUC 0.920 on phone photos — as strong as v1 ever was in its own domain — with specificity back to 75%. That 0.920 is the number that describes what the app can actually do.
knownGaps: A Fitzpatrick fairness audit (below on the page) found the model catches 94% of cancers on lighter skin but only 78% on medium skin — and the dataset holds too little darker skin (3 images, no Fitzpatrick VI) to test the group that matters most. Closing that needs more diverse data, not a modelling trick. The smartphone-trained model also still needs to be shipped into the app in place of v1.

changed: First end-to-end build: YOLO11s-cls fine-tuned for 40 epochs on 11,720 ISIC dermatoscopic images.
Exported to TensorFlow Lite and wired into the Flutter app and this in-browser demo (fully on-device).
Decision threshold deliberately set to 0.137 instead of the default 0.5, to prioritise catching malignant lesions.
improved: Established the baseline: ROC-AUC 0.914 on held-out data.
Catches 90% of malignant lesions (sensitivity) while still clearing 75% of benign ones — far better than the 'always benign' 80%-accuracy trap.
knownGaps: It was trained on dermatoscopic images, not phone photos — so its 0.914 was measured on a domain the app never sees. v2 (above) quantifies that gap (a drop to 0.743) and closes it.
